headlines.title: U.S. stocks drift higher ahead of Trump's deadline to bomb Iranian power plants
Oil edges up in choppy trade; US, Iran receive ceasefire proposal framework
Oil Prices Fluctuate as Trump's Deadline on Hormuz Looms
Wall Street 'Fear Gauge' Retreats as Geopolitical Tensions Ease
Crude prices jump sharply: WTI at $114, Brent at $111 on renewed geopolitical risk, supply concerns

notes: U.S. equities closed slightly higher on April 6, 2026, as the market balanced a potential 45-day ceasefire proposal against an impending deadline set by President Trump to bomb Iranian infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened.
Oil markets exhibited elevated volatility, with Brent crude briefly soaring above $111 before paring gains, as shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains drastically reduced.
